entities.name: FINGER MILLET
entities.description: A nutritious and easily storable grain important for food security in Africa and South Asia.

entities.name: AFRICA
entities.description: Region where finger millet was domesticated and is currently grown.

entities.name: SOUTH ASIA
entities.description: Region where finger millet is important for food security.

entities.name: GLOBAL SOUTH
entities.description: Region where finger millet is a sustainable crop for smallholders.

entities.name: IRON AGE
entities.description: Time period approximately 900–700 cal BP associated with the studied grain assemblage.

entities.name: KAKAPEL ROCK SHELTER
entities.description: Archaeological site in western Kenya where Iron Age finger millet grains were recovered.

entities.name: WESTERN KENYA
entities.description: Region in Africa and a biodiversity hotspot for finger millet landraces.

entities.name: CARBONIZATION EXPERIMENTS
entities.description: Experiments conducted on modern grains to compare archaeological specimens to extant landraces.

entities.name: CARBONIZATION TEMPERATURE
entities.description: Temperature threshold below 220 °C required for the good preservation of finger millet grains.

entities.name: GRAIN SIZE
entities.description: Morphological characteristic that varies between Iron Age and modern finger millet landraces.

entities.name: GRAIN SHAPE
entities.description: Morphological characteristic used to identify domesticated finger millet.

entities.name: SURFACE TEXTURE
entities.description: Morphological characteristic used to identify domesticated finger millet.

entities.name: QUANTITATIVE MORPHOMETRIC ANALYSIS
entities.description: The largest analysis of finger millet grains conducted to provide a basis for interpreting archaeological populations.

entities.name: FOOD SECURITY
entities.description: The state of having reliable access to sufficient nutritious food, supported by crops like finger millet.
